$schema_note: Generated from MudManager::Primitives via MudManager::Mcp::ToolSpec. Ruby is canonical — regenerate with `mud-manager --dump-spec`. Do not hand-edit.

look.description: Look at the current room or at a specific target. Call with NO arguments to describe the current room (do NOT pass target: 'room'). Pass a target to inspect a specific item, mob, or player. Use preposition 'in' to look inside a container, 'at' to inspect, or a direction to peek into an adjacent room.

target.description: Item, mob, or player to inspect. Omit to describe the current room.

preposition.description: Preposition or direction to look through.

inspect.description: Survey a room in a SINGLE call: runs `look` (room description) AND `exits` (visible exits) and returns both together. Prefer this over calling look and then exits separately when you arrive somewhere new and need to decide where to go — it gets the traversal information in one turn instead of two. Takes no arguments.

examine.description: Examine a target in detail (more verbose than look).

target.description: The item, mob, or player to examine

check.description: Query information about your character or surroundings.

move.description: Move in a compass direction or up/down.

flee.description: Attempt to flee from combat in a random available direction.

set_position.description: Change body position. Use 'rest'/'sleep' between fights to recover HP and mana. Must be standing to move or fight.

track.description: Track a mob or player by name, revealing their direction. Requires the Track skill.

target.description: Name of the mob or player to track

attack.description: Attack a target. 'kill' is standard; 'murder' bypasses the mercy check; 'hit' is a one-off strike.

target.description: Name of the mob or player to attack

skill_strike.description: Use a combat skill against a target.

target.description: Name of the mob or player

consider.description: Assess a mob's relative strength before fighting. Always consider before attacking an unknown mob.

target.description: Name of the mob to consider

say.description: Speak or emote in the current room.

tell.description: Send a private message to a specific player.

channel_say.description: Broadcast a message over a global channel.

get_item.description: Pick up an item from the room or from a container.

item.description: Name of the item to get

drop_item.description: Drop, donate, or junk an item.

put_item.description: Put an item into a container.

item.description: Name of the item to put

equip_item.description: Wear, wield, hold, grab, or remove an item.

body_loc.description: Body location to wear on (optional, e.g. 'head', 'finger')

consume_item.description: Eat, drink, taste, or sip a consumable item.

item.description: Name of the item to consume

cast_spell.description: Cast a spell, optionally at a target.

use_magic_item.description: Activate a magic item: quaff a potion, recite a scroll, or use a wand/staff.

item.description: Name of the item to activate

target_args.description: Optional target arguments (e.g. mob name for a wand)

shop.description: Interact with a shop NPC: list stock, buy, sell, or value an item.

practice.description: List your known skills at a guildmaster, or practice a specific skill.

skill.description: Skill name to practice (omit to list all)

save_character.description: Save your character to disk so progress is not lost on disconnect.

send_raw.description: Send an arbitrary command string to the MUD and return the response. Escape hatch for when no structured tool fits.

command.description: The raw command to send (e.g. 'who', 'help backstab')

poll.description: Return any unprompted output that has arrived since the last command (combat ticks, other players, room events) without sending anything. Use this to notice things that happen while idle.

mud_status.description: Report whether the MUD session is currently connected.
